description:
validate_key_type() checks key type (RSA vs ECC) and curve/key-size but does not check the key's signing scheme. An RSA-2048 key created with RSASSA (PKCS#1 v1.5) passes validation when the URI says algorithm=rsa-pss-2048. Similarly, an ECDAA key on P-256 would pass the ECDSA-P256 check.

Impact: sign() passes SignatureScheme::Null, so the TPM uses the key's default scheme. If the key's scheme is RSASSA, the TPM produces an RSASSA signature. normalize_signature then matches on TpmSig::RsaPss and falls through to the catch-all arm, returning AlgorithmMismatch with a confusing message like 'expected rsa-pss-2048, got RsaSsa' rather than stating the root cause (wrong signing scheme on the key).

For callers, validate_key_type is the place where this should be caught with a clear error. The current error path works (no silent misbehavior) but the error message is misleading — it implies the algorithm label is wrong rather than the TPM key being provisioned with the wrong scheme.

Fix: check parameters.rsa_scheme() for RSA keys and parameters.ecc_scheme() for ECC keys inside validate_key_type.

Location: secretx-tpm2/src/lib.rs lines 533-570 (validate_key_type).
title: validate_key_type skips signing scheme check
